# zerp consumer instructions for LLMs

`zerp` is a zero-config HTML presentation framework. When you are authoring a deck that depends on this package, treat the deck source of truth as `slides/`.

## Deck contract

- Put authored slides in `slides/**/*.html`.
- Files are ordered lexicographically. Use numeric prefixes like `00-`, `10-`, `20-`.
- Put deck assets under `slides/` too, for example `slides/images/cover.jpg`.
- Use relative asset paths inside slide HTML, for example `src="./images/cover.jpg"` or `src="images/cover.jpg"`.
- Each file may contain one or more `<div class="slide">` blocks.
- Do not hand-author built `index.html`; `zerp` generates it.

## Authoring style

- Prefer semantic HTML inside each `.slide`: headings, paragraphs, lists, tables, images.
- Keep slide markup self-contained. Inline styles are allowed for deck-specific presentation details, but prefer the built-in classes first.
- Assume the framework already injects fonts, navigation UI, progress bar, slide counter, CSS, and browser runtime.

## Built-in CSS classes

Use these before inventing new styling patterns:

- `.slide` for each slide root.
- `.accent`, `.accent-green`, `.accent-orange`, `.accent-purple`, `.accent-red` for emphasis colors.
- `.big-number` for large numeric callouts.
- `.two-col` for balanced two-column layouts.
- `.img-row` for rows of images.
- `.caption` for image captions and secondary labels.
- `.block-label` for small section labels in the top-left area.
- `.interactive-badge` for marking interactive slides.
- `.timeline` and `.timeline .item` with child `.year` and `.label` for milestone layouts.
- `.quote` for highlighted quotations or callouts.
- `.key-thought` for boxed takeaways.
- `.grid-demo`, `.grid-demo .cell`, `.grid-demo .cell.filled` for simple grid visualizations.

## Interaction model

`zerp` handles deck navigation. Do not recreate next/previous slide logic inside slide code.

Available deck controls:

- Right arrow, space, and PageDown move to the next slide.
- Left arrow and PageUp move to the previous slide.
- Down arrow dispatches a `slide-next` event to the current slide.
- Up arrow dispatches a `slide-prev` event to the current slide.
- Home jumps to the first slide.
- End jumps to the last slide.

## Interactive slide pattern

For stepwise reveals or custom interactions, attach listeners to the current slide element and respond to `slide-next` and `slide-prev`.

Pattern:

```html
<div class="slide">
  <div class="interactive-badge">Interactive</div>
  <h2>Example</h2>
  <div id="demo-output"></div>

  <script>
    (function () {
      var slide = document.currentScript.closest(".slide");
      var step = 0;

      function render() {
        slide.querySelector("#demo-output").textContent = "Step " + step;
      }

      slide.addEventListener("slide-next", function () {
        if (step < 3) {
          step++;
          render();
        }
      });

      slide.addEventListener("slide-prev", function () {
        if (step > 0) {
          step--;
          render();
        }
      });

      render();
    })();
  </script>
</div>
```

## Interactive slide rules

- Scope DOM queries to `document.currentScript.closest(".slide")` so multiple slides can coexist safely.
- Keep state inside the slide-local closure.
- Make `slide-prev` reverse or reset interaction state when reasonable.
- Do not attach global keyboard listeners from slide scripts unless there is a strong reason.
- Avoid dependencies on build tools or frameworks inside slide HTML.

## Good defaults

- Use `.block-label` for section context on non-title slides.
- Use `.interactive-badge` on slides that expect `slide-next` / `slide-prev` stepping.
- Use `.key-thought` for one core takeaway rather than inventing a new box style.
- Use `.two-col` instead of ad hoc layout wrappers when a balanced split is enough.

## What to avoid

- Do not embed a full HTML document inside slide files.
- Do not duplicate the framework CSS or runtime.
- Do not rely on a manually curated slide list; ordering comes from filenames.
- Do not put assets outside `slides/` unless the deck explicitly needs that and the surrounding repo is set up for it.

## Validation

When working inside the `zerp` repo itself, validate with:

```bash
pnpm build
pnpm check
pnpm lint
pnpm format:check
```

When working in a consumer deck repo, use that repo's normal `zerp build` or `zerp serve` workflow.
